2604.11674.title: AffordSim: A Scalable Data Generator and Benchmark for Affordance-Aware Robotic Manipulation
2604.11674.paper_summary: Simulation-based data generation has become a dominant paradigm for training robotic manipulation policies, yet existing platforms do not incorporate object affordance information into trajectory generation. As a result, tasks requiring precise interaction with specific functional regions--grasping a mug by its handle, pouring from a cup's rim, or hanging a mug on a hook--cannot be automatically generated with semantically correct trajectories. We introduce AffordSim, the first simulation framework that integrates open-vocabulary 3D affordance prediction into the manipulation data generation pipeline. AffordSim uses our VoxAfford model, an open-vocabulary 3D affordance detector that enhances MLLM output tokens with multi-scale geometric features, to predict affordance maps on object point clouds, guiding grasp pose estimation toward task-relevant functional regions. Built on NVIDIA Isaac Sim with cross-embodiment support (Franka FR3, Panda, UR5e, Kinova), VLM-powered task generation, and novel domain randomization using DA3-based 3D Gaussian reconstruction from real photographs, AffordSim enables automated, scalable generation of affordance-aware manipulation data. We establish a benchmark of 50 tasks across 7 categories (grasping, placing, stacking, pushing/pulling, pouring, mug hanging, long-horizon composite) and evaluate 4 imitation learning baselines (BC, Diffusion Policy, ACT, Pi 0.5). Our results reveal that while grasping is largely solved (53-93% success), affordance-demanding tasks such as pouring into narrow containers (1-43%) and mug hanging (0-47%) remain significantly more challenging for current imitation learning methods, highlighting the need for affordance-aware data generation. Zero-shot sim-to-real experiments on a real Franka FR3 validate the transferability of the generated data.
2604.11674.paper_summary_zh: 模擬基礎的數據生成已成為訓練機器人操作策略的主流範式，然而現有的平台並未將物體可供性信息納入軌跡生成中。<lb/>因此，需要與特定功能區域進行精確互動的任務——例如通過把手抓取杯子、從杯緣倒水或將杯子掛在鉤子上——無法自動生成語義正確的軌跡。<lb/>我們介紹了AffordSim，第一個將開放詞彙3D可供性預測整合到操作數據生成管道中的模擬框架。<lb/>AffordSim使用我們的VoxAfford模型，這是一個開放詞彙的3D可供性檢測器，通過多尺度幾何特徵增強MLLM輸出標記，以預測物體點雲上的可供性圖，指導抓取姿勢估計朝向任務相關的功能區域。<lb/>AffordSim基於NVIDIA Isaac Sim，支持跨實體（Franka FR3、Panda、UR5e、Kinova），具備VLM驅動的任務生成，以及使用基於DA3的從真實照片中重建的3D高斯隨機化，實現了可自動化、可擴展的可供性感知操作數據生成。<lb/>我們建立了50個任務的基準，涵蓋7個類別（抓取、放置、堆疊、推/拉、倒水、杯子懸掛、長期復合），並評估了4個模仿學習基準（BC、擴散策略、ACT、Pi 0.5）。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，雖然抓取任務基本上已解決（成功率53-93%），但對可供性要求較高的任務，如向狹窄容器倒水（1-43%）和杯子懸掛（0-47%），對當前的模仿學習方法仍然具有顯著挑戰性，突顯了可供性感知數據生成的必要性。<lb/>在真實的Franka FR3上進行的零-shot模擬到現實實驗驗證了生成數據的可轉移性。
2604.11674.author: Mingyang Li et.al.
2604.11674.authors: Mingyang Li, Haofan Xu, Haowen Sun, Xinzhe Chen, Sihua Ren, Liqi Huang, Xinyang Sui, Chenyang Miao, Qiongjie Cui, Zeyang Liu, Xingyu Chen, Xuguang Lan
